current_risk_from_prior_evidence.risk: RPC body is live-equivalent, but EXECUTE exposure includes PUBLIC and anon; service boundary is not canonically locked.

governance_canon: RPCs callable by anon must be intentionally public and safe.
SECURITY DEFINER functions must use minimal executable roles and REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC unless intentionally public.
Direct private/helper behavior must not be exposed through broad public RPC access.
economic_constraint_model: Energy daily sync is user-scoped state orchestration that may affect Flow, reward, or economy-adjacent progression state.
Economy-adjacent synchronization must be owned by a trusted server boundary, not arbitrary browser or anon execution.
The least-risk posture is service_role-only execution from Worker/API, Express/AIER, or Edge Function internals after explicit authorization.
runtime_ownership_model: Service role is server-only and forbidden in browser exposure.
Browser direct Supabase calls are allowed only for explicit RLS/RPC/read contracts.
Wallet/reward/energy transactional or synchronization work belongs behind Worker/API or Edge Function boundaries, not public/anon callable RPCs.

future_sql_decision_not_applied: REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.rpc_sync_energy_daily(uuid,date) FROM PUBLIC;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.rpc_sync_energy_daily(uuid,date) FROM anon;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.rpc_sync_energy_daily(uuid,date) FROM authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.rpc_sync_energy_daily(uuid,date) TO service_role;

Confirm owning server runtime path for energy daily sync.
Run dry transaction with privilege assertions.
Verify service_role can execute from approved server boundary.
Run affected runtime smoke without exposing service credentials.
